content: The Evangelism Explosion model begins with two diagnostic questions. The first asks: "Have you come to the place in your spiritual life where you know for certain that if you were to die today you would go to Heaven?" This establishes the Bible as our authority. The second asks what they would say if God asked why He should let them into Heaven, revealing if they are trusting in works. Jump to [[1 John 5:13]]

The first point is Grace. Heaven is a free gift. Religion teaches that people must earn God's favor, but grace means God's favor can only be obtained by receiving it as a free gift. Jump to [[Romans 6:23]]

Because Heaven is a gift, it cannot be earned or deserved. "Friend's Gift" illustration: If a friend surprises you with a car and you pay a dollar for it, it becomes an insult. It is no longer a gift. Why isn't it earned? Because of what the Bible says about man. Jump to [[Ephesians 2:8]]
The second point is Man. Man is a sinner. Sin is defined not just as bad acts, but anything that misses God's standard. Jump to [[Romans 3:23]]

Because man is a sinner, he cannot save himself. You must simply be as good as God. "Bad Egg" illustration: If you make an omelet with three eggs, but one is rotten, it is no longer a perfect omelet. We can't save ourselves because of what the Bible teaches about God. Jump to [[Matthew 5:48]]
The third point is God. God is merciful and therefore doesn't want to punish us. This is the "Good News" that God is a loving God. Jump to [[1 John 4:8]]

While God is merciful, He is also just and must punish our sin. "Bank Robber" illustration: If a judge lets a guilty robber go free just because he apologizes, the judge is not just. God solved this problem in the person of Jesus Christ. Jump to [[Exodus 34:7]]
The fourth point is Christ. First, we show who Jesus Christ is: He is both 100% God and 100% man. Jump to [[John 1:1]]

Second, we show what Jesus Christ did. He died on the cross and rose from the dead to pay the penalty for our sins. "Record Book" illustration: God placed all of our sin from the record book on His Son. We receive this gift through faith. Jump to [[Isaiah 53:6]]

The final point is Faith. Saving faith is not mere intellectual assent; even the demons believe and tremble. It is also not temporal faith, which is just trusting God for temporary crisis needs. Jump to [[James 2:19]]

True saving faith is trusting in Jesus Christ alone for our eternal life. "Boat" illustration: If you are drowning, you have to let go of your little piece of wood to grab the Coast Guard's life preserver. We must let go of trusting our own works. Jump to [[Acts 16:31]]

Ask: "Does this make sense to you?" followed by: "Would you like to receive God's free gift of eternal life?" After praying, use this verse for Assurance. Jesus says the one who believes has everlasting life.
